title: pterostylis major
intro: Pterostylis major, commonly known as the greenhood orchid, is a species of orchid native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The rhizomes and roots of Pterostylis major have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal benefits.
